claims.text: Anthropic received a US government export control directive on June 12, 2026 at 5:21pm ET ordering suspension of all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national
Both Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were disabled for ALL customers globally because Anthropic cannot selectively block foreign nationals within its customer base
Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are the same underlying model; Fable 5 has safety classifiers that route cybersecurity, biology/chemistry, and distillation queries to Opus 4.8
The government's stated concern was a specific jailbreak technique used to identify a small number of previously known, minor vulnerabilities
No universal jailbreak was found across over 1,000 hours of internal and external red-teaming; Fable 5 complied with zero harmful single-turn cyber requests across 30 public jailbreak techniques
Anthropic voluntarily stopped selling to groups majority-owned by Chinese, Russian, Iranian, or North Korean entities in September 2025
The Trump administration ordered US government agencies to stop using Anthropic models on February 27, 2026, and the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a 'supply chain risk'
A federal judge granted a temporary injunction against the Pentagon actions on March 26, 2026, writing the case 'appears to be classic First Amendment retaliation'
Mythos Preview scored 83.1% on CyberGym (vs 66.6% for Claude Opus 4.6) and 77.8% on SWE-bench Pro (vs 53.4%)
Project Glasswing partners found more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across systemically important software
Anthropic stated 'perfect jailbreak resistance is not currently possible for any model provider' and that Fable 5's defense-in-depth approach makes risks comparable to existing models
Anthropic argues the action sets a precedent that 'would essentially halt all new model deployments for all frontier model providers' if applied consistently
The Bernstein v. United States litigation established encryption source code as speech protected by the First Amendment
